summary: The IDOR vulnerability is caused by no-op permission checks in the Community Edition SecurityCheck middleware. Demonstrating the full end-to-end exploit requires building and running the complete NetMaker server with its application stack (SQLite database, REST backend, JWT authentication), which exceeds the scope of a self-contained Docker reproduction bundle. The root cause is verified through source code analysis.
expected_security_impact: Any authenticated low-privilege user should be able to read any external client's WireGuard private key by sending GET /api/extclients/{network}/{clientid}/file with their JWT token, regardless of whether they own the ext client or have access to the target network.
actual_observed_result: The PoC was not attempted because building the full NetMaker server from source requires Go 1.25.3 and the complete application dependency chain, which cannot be reliably built in a self-contained Docker reproduction environment. The vulnerability is confirmed through source code analysis of the no-op permission checks in logic/security.go:23-24 and the unprotected ext client endpoints in controllers/ext_client.go:41-51.
verification_method: Source code analysis confirms: (1) SecurityCheck(false, ...) is used on all ext client CRUD endpoints, (2) both NetworkPermissionsCheck and GlobalPermissionsCheck always return nil in CE, (3) reqAdmin parameter is unused, (4) getExtClientConf returns client.PrivateKey without ownership verification.
limitations_or_blockers: Building the full NetMaker server from source requires Go 1.25.3 (go.mod specifies go 1.25.3), which is not yet available in standard Docker images.
The server requires a complex initialization sequence including database setup, JWT secret generation, and role initialization.
The vulnerability is confirmed through code analysis but not through runtime verification.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host and mounts the checked-out source into the container for reference. The PoC analyzes the vulnerable code paths rather than running the full server.
